mappings.rationale: Section B covers statistics comprehensively including statistical inference, distribution functions, estimation, hypothesis testing, and regression analysis.
Probability underpins statistical inference (B) and mathematical optimization (E), core applied mathematics topics.
Section G covers computer science including algorithms, theory of computation, computer architecture, programming languages, and AI.
Section G.6 specifically lists artificial intelligence as a branch of computer science within applied mathematics.
Sections A.6-7 cover analogue and digital computation, and G covers computer architecture and programming.
Sections A.6-7 trace the development of analogue and digital computation, the history of computing.
Section E.1 covers the theory of games as analysis of strategic features of conflict situations.
Section F.4 specifically names cryptography as an application of information theory.
Section F covers information theory including origins, central problems, principles, and applications to cryptography and linguistics.
Directly covers the applications of mathematics to physical and other sciences, the central subject of this section.
Network theory connects to cybernetics (E.3), information theory (F), and computational methods (G).
Big data relates to statistics (B), numerical analysis (C), and computer science (G) for large-scale data analysis.
Chaos theory applies mathematical analysis to physical systems, relevant to control theory (E.4) and numerical analysis (C).
Complexity science connects to cybernetics (E.3), control theory (E.4), and computational methods (G).
Robotics draws on cybernetics (E.3), control theory (E.4), and artificial intelligence (G.6).
Mathematical finance applies probability, statistics, and optimization theory (B, E) to financial systems.
Mathematical biology applies computational models and numerical analysis (C, G) to biological systems.
Section H.6 covers relativity theory as a mathematical framework involving four-dimensional spacetime.
Section H.8 covers quantum mechanics as a mathematical aspect of physical theories.
Section H.2 specifically covers fluid mechanics as a mathematical aspect of physical theories.
Section H.7 covers Riemannian geometry as a mathematical aspect of physical theories, and A.2 covers geometrical aids.
Measurement connects to statistical sciences (B) and the mathematical methods applied to empirical observations.
Sections A.2.b-d cover geometrical aids for observation, navigation, and celestial measurement.
Fractals represent applications of mathematical analysis to geometry and physical phenomena, connecting to C and H.
Group theory and symmetry underpin the mathematical aspects of physical theories in section H.
Choice theory connects to game theory (E.1) and linear/nonlinear programming (E.2) for mathematical optimization.
Section A.4 covers calculatory aspects of algebra including algebraic notation, logarithms, and slide rules.
Section A.2.e covers optical instruments as geometrical aids, and H.5 covers electromagnetic theory in physics.
